name: style-memory-retrieval-seed
description: First deterministic retrieval experiment slice for comparing heuristic, style-memory, and hybrid reference selection before generation changes.

rationale.k: Use k=3 because drafting requires 3 to 5 references and the lower bound keeps the retrieval eval strict: a relevant style signal must appear in the compact context window, not only after extra padding.

request.angle: Share how much the current multi-agent setup costs

cases.notes: Cost/setup requests should retrieve the explicit setup-cost reference and the workflow-authority reference.

request.angle: Turn the Telegram bot launch into a grounded professional update about human-in-the-loop AI products
request.source_notes: The bot drafts posts, supports revise/approve/final feedback, and keeps publishing manual.

cases.notes: LinkedIn packaging should lean on project/product update references before borrowing tool-decision field notes.

request.angle: Explain why I dropped one AI review tool after testing it for a couple of weeks

cases.notes: Tool-decision posts should retrieve the Gemini review field note and the grounded Anthropic cost take.

request.angle: React to Claude Managed Agents and explain why I did not find a use for it after my API cost experiment

cases.notes: This case separates pure style-memory retrieval from hybrid retrieval: style-memory leans toward cost/setup similarity, while hybrid should preserve the recipe-level tool-decision reference.
